revision.comment: 大阪府河内長野市にある神社（[[:commons:Image:Takou-jinja1.jpg]]より）

revision.text:
'''高向神社'''(たこうじんじゃ)は[[大阪府]][[河内長野市]]にある神社。
祭神は、当初、[[素盞嗚尊]]、[[蛭子神]]、[[天児屋根命]]、[[保食神]]、[[白山姫命]]、[[菅原道真]]。現在は近隣の神社から[[武甕槌命]]、[[経津主命]]、[[比咩大神]]、[[大巳貴命]]が合祀されている。
創建年は不詳。本殿は[[慶長]]13年（[[1608年]]）の建立で三間社切妻造。本殿と高向神社祭礼図絵馬（[[文久]]3年([[1863年]]））は河内長野市の市指定文化財である。
氏地は河内長野市高向、日野地区である。例大祭は10月10日で、こども獅子舞、日野獅子舞（河内長野市無形民族文化財）、[[地車]]の宮入が行われる。
{{shinto-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:たこうしんしや}}
[[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内長野市]]

revision.comment: ページ [[高向神社]] を [[高向神社 (河内長野市)]] へ移動: 高向神社が複数あるので

'''高向神社'''（たこうじんじゃ）は[[大阪府]][[河内長野市]]にある神社。
祭神は、当初、[[素盞嗚尊]]、[[蛭子神]]、[[天児屋根命]]、[[保食神]]、[[白山姫命]]、[[菅原道真]]。現在は近隣の神社から[[武甕槌命]]、[[経津主命]]、[[比咩大神]]、[[大巳貴命]]が合祀されている。
創建年は不詳。本殿は[[慶長]]13年（[[1608年]]）の建立で三間社切妻造。本殿と高向神社祭礼図絵馬（[[文久]]3年([[1863年]]））は河内長野市の市指定文化財である。

{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たこうしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内長野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内長野市の建築物]]

{{神社 |名称=高向神社 |画像=[[File:Takou-jinja1.jpg|250px]]
社殿正面 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[河内長野市]][[高向]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 26 | 緯度秒 =8 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 32 | 経度秒 = 56.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Osaka |祭神=[[素盞嗚尊]]
[[蛭子神]]
[[天児屋根命]]
[[保食神]]
[[菊理媛神|白山姫命]]
[[菅原道真]] |社格= |創建=不詳 |本殿=三間社[[切妻造]] |別名= 五社明神（旧称） |札所等= |例祭=10月10日に獅子舞、地車 |神事= }} '''高向神社'''（たこうじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[河内長野市]]にある神社。 ==祭神== 祭神は、当初、[[素盞嗚尊]]、[[蛭子神]]、[[天児屋根命]]、[[保食神]]、[[菊理媛神|白山姫命]]、[[菅原道真]]。
現在は近隣の神社から[[武甕槌命]]、[[経津主命]]、[[比咩大神]]、[[大巳貴命]]が合祀されている。 ==歴史== 創建年は不詳。本殿は[[1608年]]（[[慶長]]13年）の建立で三間社切妻造。本殿と[[1863年]]（[[文久]]3年）に作られた高向神社祭礼図絵馬は河内長野市の市指定文化財に指定されている。[[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）に日野地区にあった春日神社の[[武甕槌命]]、[[経津主命]]、[[比咩大神]]と八坂神社の[[大巳貴命]]が合祀された。 == 祭事 == 氏地は河内長野市[[高向|高向、日野]]地区である。例大祭は10月10日で、こども獅子舞、日野獅子舞（河内長野市無形民俗文化財）、[[地車]]の宮入が行われる。 == 文化財 == ;河内長野市指定有形民俗文化財 *高向神社本殿（江戸時代初期の建立） - 昭和51年3月30日指定 *女神、牛頭天王、男神の各坐像（木造・女神と牛頭天王は鎌倉期の作、男神は室町期の作） - 昭和45年3月27日指定 *高向神社祭礼図絵馬（文久3年の作） - 昭和57年3月19日指定 ;河内長野市指定無形民俗文化財 *日野地区獅子舞（江戸時代から） - 昭和59年3月29日指定 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[河内長野市]][[高向]]291番地 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[南海高野線]]及び[[近鉄長野線]][[河内長野駅]]下車後[[南海バス]]高向線に乗車し、バス停「上高向」下車から徒歩約3分（南へ約230m） == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_02_kawatinagano_takou.html 高向神社 - 大阪府神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たこうしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内長野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内長野市の建築物]]

revision.comment: Created page with "'''Takō Shrine''' (高向神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. == Deities Enshrined == The shrine originally enshrined {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子神}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食神}}, {{ill|Kukurihime|ja|菊理媛神}} (Shirayama-hime no Mikoto), and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|..."
revision.text:
'''Takō Shrine''' (高向神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Deities Enshrined == The shrine originally enshrined {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子神}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食神}}, {{ill|Kukurihime|ja|菊理媛神}} (Shirayama-hime no Mikoto), and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}.
Later, the shrine incorporated additional deities from nearby shrines, including {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, {{ill|Hime-no-Okami|ja|比咩大神}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大巳貴命}}.
== History == The exact year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. The main hall was constructed in [[1608]] ({{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 13) and features a three-bay gabled roof style (三間社切妻造).
The main hall and a votive ema depicting the Takō Shrine festival, created in [[1863]] ({{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3), have been designated as cultural properties by the city of Kawachinagano. In [[1908]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 41), the shrine merged with the Spring Shrine (春日神社) from the Hino district, enshrining {{ill|Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|Futsunushi}}, and {{ill|Hime-no-Okami}}. It also incorporated {{ill|Ōkuninushi}} from Yasaka Shrine.
== Festivals == The shrine's parish covers the Takō and Hino districts of Kawachinagano. The annual festival takes place on October 10 and includes a children's lion dance, the Hino lion dance (a Kawachinagano City-designated intangible folk cultural property), and the ceremonial entry of {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} floats into the shrine.
== Cultural Properties == === Kawachinagano City-Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Main Hall**: Constructed in the early Edo period, designated on March 30, 1976. * **Wooden Statues**: Includes a goddess, Gozu Tennō, and a male deity. The goddess and Gozu Tennō statues date back to the Kamakura period, while the male deity statue is from the Muromachi period. Designated on March 27, 1970. * **Takō Shrine Festival Ema**: Created in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), designated on March 19, 1982.
=== Kawachinagano City-Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * **Hino Lion Dance**: A practice dating back to the Edo period, designated on March 29, 1984.
== Access == ;Location * 291 Takō, Kawachinagano, Osaka Prefecture, Japan
;Transportation * The nearest station is {{ill|Kawachinagano Station|ja|河内長野駅}}, accessible via the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線}}. From the station, take the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス}} Takō Line and get off at the "Kamidakō" bus stop. The shrine is about a 3-minute walk south (approximately 230 meters).

[[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kawachinagano]] [[ja:高向神社]]

'''Takō Shrine''' (高向神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Deities Enshrined == The shrine originally enshrined {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子神}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食神}}, {{ill|Kukurihime|ja|菊理媛神}} (Shirayama-hime no Mikoto), and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}.
Later, the shrine incorporated additional deities from nearby shrines, including {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, {{ill|Hime-no-Okami|ja|比咩大神}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大巳貴命}}.
== History == The exact year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. The main hall was constructed in [[1608]] ({{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 13) and features a three-bay gabled roof style (三間社切妻造).
The main hall and a votive ema depicting the Takō Shrine festival, created in [[1863]] ({{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3), have been designated as cultural properties by the city of Kawachinagano. In [[1908]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 41), the shrine merged with the Spring Shrine (春日神社) from the Hino district, enshrining {{ill|Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|Futsunushi}}, and {{ill|Hime-no-Okami}}. It also incorporated {{ill|Ōkuninushi}} from Yasaka Shrine.
== Festivals == The shrine's parish covers the Takō and Hino districts of Kawachinagano. The annual festival takes place on October 10 and includes a children's lion dance, the Hino lion dance (a Kawachinagano City-designated intangible folk cultural property), and the ceremonial entry of {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} floats into the shrine.
== Cultural Properties == === Kawachinagano City-Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Main Hall**: Constructed in the early Edo period, designated on March 30, 1976. * **Wooden Statues**: Includes a goddess, Gozu Tennō, and a male deity. The goddess and Gozu Tennō statues date back to the Kamakura period, while the male deity statue is from the Muromachi period. Designated on March 27, 1970. * **Takō Shrine Festival Ema**: Created in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), designated on March 19, 1982.
=== Kawachinagano City-Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * **Hino Lion Dance**: A practice dating back to the Edo period, designated on March 29, 1984.
== Access == ;Location * 291 Takō, Kawachinagano, Osaka Prefecture, Japan
;Transportation * The nearest station is {{ill|Kawachinagano Station|ja|河内長野駅}}, accessible via the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線}}. From the station, take the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス}} Takō Line and get off at the "Kamidakō" bus stop. The shrine is about a 3-minute walk south (approximately 230 meters).
== External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_02_kawatinagano_takou.html Takō Shrine - Osaka Shrine Association (大阪府神社庁)]
{{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}}

revision.text:
'''Takō Shrine''' (高向神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Deities Enshrined == The shrine originally enshrined {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子神}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食神}}, {{ill|Kukurihime|ja|菊理媛神}} (Shirayama-hime no Mikoto), and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}.
Later, the shrine incorporated additional deities from nearby shrines, including {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami|ja|比咩大神}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大巳貴命}}.
== History == The exact year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. The main hall was constructed in [[1608]] ({{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 13) and features a three-bay gabled roof style (三間社切妻造).
The main hall and a votive ema depicting the Takō Shrine festival, created in [[1863]] ({{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3), have been designated as cultural properties by the city of Kawachinagano. In [[1908]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 41), the shrine merged with the Spring Shrine (春日神社) from the Hino district, enshrining {{ill|Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|Futsunushi}}, and {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami}}. It also incorporated {{ill|Ōkuninushi}} from Yasaka Shrine.
== Festivals == The shrine's parish covers the Takō and Hino districts of Kawachinagano. The annual festival takes place on October 10 and includes a children's lion dance, the Hino lion dance (a Kawachinagano City-designated intangible folk cultural property), and the ceremonial entry of {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} floats into the shrine.
== Cultural Properties == === Kawachinagano City-Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Main Hall**: Constructed in the early Edo period, designated on March 30, 1976. * **Wooden Statues**: Includes a goddess, Gozu Tennō, and a male deity. The goddess and Gozu Tennō statues date back to the Kamakura period, while the male deity statue is from the Muromachi period. Designated on March 27, 1970. * **Takō Shrine Festival Ema**: Created in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), designated on March 19, 1982.
=== Kawachinagano City-Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * **Hino Lion Dance**: A practice dating back to the Edo period, designated on March 29, 1984.
== Access == ;Location * 291 Takō, Kawachinagano, Osaka Prefecture, Japan
;Transportation * The nearest station is {{ill|Kawachinagano Station|ja|河内長野駅}}, accessible via the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線}}. From the station, take the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス}} Takō Line and get off at the "Kamidakō" bus stop. The shrine is about a 3-minute walk south (approximately 230 meters).
== External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_02_kawatinagano_takou.html Takō Shrine - Osaka Shrine Association (大阪府神社庁)]

'''Takō Shrine''' (高向神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
== Deities Enshrined == The shrine originally enshrined {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子神}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食神}}, {{ill|Kukurihime|ja|菊理媛神}} (Shirayama-hime no Mikoto), and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}.
Later, the shrine incorporated additional deities from nearby shrines, including {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami|ja|比咩大神}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大巳貴命}}.
== History == The exact year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. The main hall was constructed in [[1608]] ({{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 13) and features a three-bay gabled roof style (三間社切妻造).
The main hall and a votive ema depicting the Takō Shrine festival, created in [[1863]] ({{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3), have been designated as cultural properties by the city of Kawachinagano. In [[1908]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 41), the shrine merged with the Spring Shrine (春日神社) from the Hino district, enshrining {{ill|Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|Futsunushi}}, and {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami}}. It also incorporated {{ill|Ōkuninushi}} from Yasaka Shrine.
== Festivals == The shrine's parish covers the Takō and Hino districts of Kawachinagano. The annual festival takes place on October 10 and includes a children's lion dance, the Hino lion dance (a Kawachinagano City-designated intangible folk cultural property), and the ceremonial entry of {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} floats into the shrine.
== Cultural Properties == === Kawachinagano City-Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Main Hall**: Constructed in the early Edo period, designated on March 30, 1976. * **Wooden Statues**: Includes a goddess, Gozu Tennō, and a male deity. The goddess and Gozu Tennō statues date back to the Kamakura period, while the male deity statue is from the Muromachi period. Designated on March 27, 1970. * **Takō Shrine Festival Ema**: Created in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), designated on March 19, 1982.
=== Kawachinagano City-Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * **Hino Lion Dance**: A practice dating back to the Edo period, designated on March 29, 1984.
== Access == ;Location * 291 Takō, Kawachinagano, Osaka Prefecture, Japan
;Transportation * The nearest station is {{ill|Kawachinagano Station|ja|河内長野駅}}, accessible via the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線}}. From the station, take the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス}} Takō Line and get off at the "Kamidakō" bus stop. The shrine is about a 3-minute walk south (approximately 230 meters).
== External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_02_kawatinagano_takou.html Takō Shrine - Osaka Shrine Association (大阪府神社庁)]
{{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}}

'''Takō Shrine''' (高向神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. [[ja:高向神社_(河内長野市)]] == Deities Enshrined == The shrine originally enshrined {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子神}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食神}}, {{ill|Kukurihime|ja|菊理媛神}} (Shirayama-hime no Mikoto), and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}.
Later, the shrine incorporated additional deities from nearby shrines, including {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami|ja|比咩大神}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大巳貴命}}.
== History == The exact year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. The main hall was constructed in [[1608]] ({{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 13) and features a three-bay gabled roof style (三間社切妻造).
The main hall and a votive ema depicting the Takō Shrine festival, created in [[1863]] ({{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3), have been designated as cultural properties by the city of Kawachinagano. In [[1908]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 41), the shrine merged with the Spring Shrine (春日神社) from the Hino district, enshrining {{ill|Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|Futsunushi}}, and {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami}}. It also incorporated {{ill|Ōkuninushi}} from Yasaka Shrine.
== Festivals == The shrine's parish covers the Takō and Hino districts of Kawachinagano. The annual festival takes place on October 10 and includes a children's lion dance, the Hino lion dance (a Kawachinagano City-designated intangible folk cultural property), and the ceremonial entry of {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} floats into the shrine.
== Cultural Properties == === Kawachinagano City-Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Main Hall**: Constructed in the early Edo period, designated on March 30, 1976. * **Wooden Statues**: Includes a goddess, Gozu Tennō, and a male deity. The goddess and Gozu Tennō statues date back to the Kamakura period, while the male deity statue is from the Muromachi period. Designated on March 27, 1970. * **Takō Shrine Festival Ema**: Created in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), designated on March 19, 1982.
=== Kawachinagano City-Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * **Hino Lion Dance**: A practice dating back to the Edo period, designated on March 29, 1984.
== Access == ;Location * 291 Takō, Kawachinagano, Osaka Prefecture, Japan
;Transportation * The nearest station is {{ill|Kawachinagano Station|ja|河内長野駅}}, accessible via the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線}}. From the station, take the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス}} Takō Line and get off at the "Kamidakō" bus stop. The shrine is about a 3-minute walk south (approximately 230 meters).
== External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_02_kawatinagano_takou.html Takō Shrine - Osaka Shrine Association (大阪府神社庁)]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=高向神社 |画像=[[File:Takou-jinja1.jpg|250px]]
社殿正面 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[河内長野市]][[高向]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 26 | 緯度秒 =8 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 32 | 経度秒 = 56.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Osaka |祭神=[[素盞嗚尊]]
[[蛭子神]]
[[天児屋根命]]
[[保食神]]
[[菊理媛神|白山姫命]]
[[菅原道真]] |社格= |創建=不詳 |本殿=三間社[[切妻造]] |別名= 五社明神（旧称） |札所等= |例祭=10月10日に獅子舞、地車 |神事= }} '''Takō Shrine''' (高向神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. [[ja:高向神社_(河内長野市)]] == Deities Enshrined == The shrine originally enshrined {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子神}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食神}}, {{ill|Kukurihime|ja|菊理媛神}} (Shirayama-hime no Mikoto), and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. Later, the shrine incorporated additional deities from nearby shrines, including {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami|ja|比咩大神}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大巳貴命}}. == History == The exact year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. The main hall was constructed in [[1608]] ({{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 13) and features a three-bay gabled roof style (三間社切妻造). The main hall and a votive ema depicting the Takō Shrine festival, created in [[1863]] ({{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3), have been designated as cultural properties by the city of Kawachinagano. In [[1908]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 41), the shrine merged with the Spring Shrine (春日神社) from the Hino district, enshrining {{ill|Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|Futsunushi}}, and {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami}}. It also incorporated {{ill|Ōkuninushi}} from Yasaka Shrine. == Festivals == The shrine's parish covers the Takō and Hino districts of Kawachinagano. The annual festival takes place on October 10 and includes a children's lion dance, the Hino lion dance (a Kawachinagano City-designated intangible folk cultural property), and the ceremonial entry of {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} floats into the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Kawachinagano City-Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Main Hall**: Constructed in the early Edo period, designated on March 30, 1976. * **Wooden Statues**: Includes a goddess, Gozu Tennō, and a male deity. The goddess and Gozu Tennō statues date back to the Kamakura period, while the male deity statue is from the Muromachi period. Designated on March 27, 1970. * **Takō Shrine Festival Ema**: Created in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), designated on March 19, 1982. === Kawachinagano City-Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * **Hino Lion Dance**: A practice dating back to the Edo period, designated on March 29, 1984. == Access == ;Location * 291 Takō, Kawachinagano, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ;Transportation * The nearest station is {{ill|Kawachinagano Station|ja|河内長野駅}}, accessible via the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線}}. From the station, take the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス}} Takō Line and get off at the "Kamidakō" bus stop. The shrine is about a 3-minute walk south (approximately 230 meters). == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_02_kawatinagano_takou.html Takō Shrine - Osaka Shrine Association (大阪府神社庁)] {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Unsorted Completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)-qq]] [[Category:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]] [[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:高向神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]]

In [[1908]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 41), the shrine merged with the Spring Shrine (春日神社) from the Hino district, enshrining {{ill|Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|Futsunushi}}, and {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami}}. It also incorporated {{ill|Ōkuninushi}} from Yasaka Shrine. == Festivals == The shrine's parish covers the Takō and Hino districts of Kawachinagano. The annual festival takes place on October 10 and includes a children's lion dance, the Hino lion dance (a Kawachinagano City-designated intangible folk cultural property), and the ceremonial entry of {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} floats into the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Kawachinagano City-Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Main Hall**: Constructed in the early Edo period, designated on March 30, 1976. * **Wooden Statues**: Includes a goddess, Gozu Tennō, and a male deity. The goddess and Gozu Tennō statues date back to the Kamakura period, while the male deity statue is from the Muromachi period. Designated on March 27, 1970. * **Takō Shrine Festival Ema**: Created in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), designated on March 19, 1982. === Kawachinagano City-Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * **Hino Lion Dance**: A practice dating back to the Edo period, designated on March 29, 1984. == Access == ;Location * 291 Takō, Kawachinagano, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ;Transportation * The nearest station is {{ill|Kawachinagano Station|ja|河内長野駅}}, accessible via the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線}}. From the station, take the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス}} Takō Line and get off at the "Kamidakō" bus stop. The shrine is about a 3-minute walk south (approximately 230 meters). == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_02_kawatinagano_takou.html Takō Shrine - Osaka Shrine Association (大阪府神社庁)] {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Unsorted Completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]]

社殿正面 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[河内長野市]][[高向]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 26 | 緯度秒 =8 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 32 | 経度秒 = 56.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Osaka |祭神=[[素盞嗚尊]]
[[蛭子神]]
[[天児屋根命]]
[[保食神]]
[[菊理媛神|白山姫命]]
[[菅原道真]] |社格= |創建=不詳 |本殿=三間社[[切妻造]] |別名= 五社明神（旧称） |札所等= |例祭=10月10日に獅子舞、地車 |神事= }} '''Takō Shrine''' (高向神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. [[ja:高向神社_(河内長野市)]] == Deities Enshrined == The shrine originally enshrined {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子神}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食神}}, {{ill|Kukurihime|ja|菊理媛神}} (Shirayama-hime no Mikoto), and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. Later, the shrine incorporated additional deities from nearby shrines, including {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami|ja|比咩大神}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大巳貴命}}. == History == The exact year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. The main hall was constructed in [[1608]] ({{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 13) and features a three-bay gabled roof style (三間社切妻造). The main hall and a votive ema depicting the Takō Shrine festival, created in [[1863]] ({{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3), have been designated as cultural properties by the city of Kawachinagano. In [[1908]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 41), the shrine merged with the Spring Shrine (春日神社) from the Hino district, enshrining {{ill|Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|Futsunushi}}, and {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami}}. It also incorporated {{ill|Ōkuninushi}} from Yasaka Shrine. == Festivals == The shrine's parish covers the Takō and Hino districts of Kawachinagano. The annual festival takes place on October 10 and includes a children's lion dance, the Hino lion dance (a Kawachinagano City-designated intangible folk cultural property), and the ceremonial entry of {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} floats into the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Kawachinagano City-Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Main Hall**: Constructed in the early Edo period, designated on March 30, 1976. * **Wooden Statues**: Includes a goddess, Gozu Tennō, and a male deity. The goddess and Gozu Tennō statues date back to the Kamakura period, while the male deity statue is from the Muromachi period. Designated on March 27, 1970. * **Takō Shrine Festival Ema**: Created in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), designated on March 19, 1982. === Kawachinagano City-Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * **Hino Lion Dance**: A practice dating back to the Edo period, designated on March 29, 1984. == Access == ;Location * 291 Takō, Kawachinagano, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ;Transportation * The nearest station is {{ill|Kawachinagano Station|ja|河内長野駅}}, accessible via the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線}}. From the station, take the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス}} Takō Line and get off at the "Kamidakō" bus stop. The shrine is about a 3-minute walk south (approximately 230 meters). == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_02_kawatinagano_takou.html Takō Shrine - Osaka Shrine Association (大阪府神社庁)] {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Unsorted Completion categories]] [[category:qq close]] [[Category:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]] [[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:高向神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]]

[[ja:高向神社_(河内長野市)]] == Deities Enshrined == The shrine originally enshrined {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子神}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}, {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食神}}, {{ill|Kukurihime|ja|菊理媛神}} (Shirayama-hime no Mikoto), and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. Later, the shrine incorporated additional deities from nearby shrines, including {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami|ja|比咩大神}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大巳貴命}}. == History == The exact year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. The main hall was constructed in [[1608]] ({{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 13) and features a three-bay gabled roof style (三間社切妻造). The main hall and a votive ema depicting the Takō Shrine festival, created in [[1863]] ({{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3), have been designated as cultural properties by the city of Kawachinagano. In [[1908]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 41), the shrine merged with the Spring Shrine (春日神社) from the Hino district, enshrining {{ill|Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|Futsunushi}}, and {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami}}. It also incorporated {{ill|Ōkuninushi}} from Yasaka Shrine. == Festivals == The shrine's parish covers the Takō and Hino districts of Kawachinagano. The annual festival takes place on October 10 and includes a children's lion dance, the Hino lion dance (a Kawachinagano City-designated intangible folk cultural property), and the ceremonial entry of {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} floats into the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Kawachinagano City-Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Main Hall**: Constructed in the early Edo period, designated on March 30, 1976. * **Wooden Statues**: Includes a goddess, Gozu Tennō, and a male deity. The goddess and Gozu Tennō statues date back to the Kamakura period, while the male deity statue is from the Muromachi period. Designated on March 27, 1970. * **Takō Shrine Festival Ema**: Created in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), designated on March 19, 1982. === Kawachinagano City-Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * **Hino Lion Dance**: A practice dating back to the Edo period, designated on March 29, 1984. == Access == ;Location * 291 Takō, Kawachinagano, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ;Transportation * The nearest station is {{ill|Kawachinagano Station|ja|河内長野駅}}, accessible via the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線}}. From the station, take the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス}} Takō Line and get off at the "Kamidakō" bus stop. The shrine is about a 3-minute walk south (approximately 230 meters). == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_02_kawatinagano_takou.html Takō Shrine - Osaka Shrine Association (大阪府神社庁)] {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kawachinagano]] [[Category:completion tier 3]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]] [[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:高向神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]]

The goddess and Gozu Tennō statues date back to the Kamakura period, while the male deity statue is from the Muromachi period. Designated on March 27, 1970. * **Takō Shrine Festival Ema**: Created in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), designated on March 19, 1982. === Kawachinagano City-Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * **Hino Lion Dance**: A practice dating back to the Edo period, designated on March 29, 1984. == Access == ;Location * 291 Takō, Kawachinagano, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ;Transportation * The nearest station is {{ill|Kawachinagano Station|ja|河内長野駅}}, accessible via the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線}}. From the station, take the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス}} Takō Line and get off at the "Kamidakō" bus stop. The shrine is about a 3-minute walk south (approximately 230 meters). == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_02_kawatinagano_takou.html Takō Shrine - Osaka Shrine Association (大阪府神社庁)] {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kawachinagano]] [[Category:completion tier 3]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]] [[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:高向神社]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=高向神社 |image=[[File:Takou-jinja1.jpg|250px]]
社殿正面 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[河内長野市]][[高向]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 26 | 緯度秒 =8 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 32 | 経度秒 = 56.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Osaka |祭神=[[素盞嗚尊]]
[[蛭子神]]
[[天児屋根命]]
[[保食神]]
[[菊理媛神|白山姫命]]
[[菅原道真]] |社格= |創建=不詳 |本殿=三間社[[切妻造]] |別名= 五社明神（旧称） |札所等= |例祭=10月10日に獅子舞、地車 |神事= }} '''Takō Shrine''' (高向神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. [[ja:高向神社_(河内長野市)]] == Deities Enshrined == The shrine originally enshrined {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子神}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食神}}, {{ill|Kukurihime|ja|菊理媛神}} (Shirayama-hime no Mikoto), and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. Later, the shrine incorporated additional deities from nearby shrines, including {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami|ja|比咩大神}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大巳貴命}}. == History == The exact year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. The main hall was constructed in [[1608]] ({{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 13) and features a three-bay gabled roof style (三間社切妻造). The main hall and a votive ema depicting the Takō Shrine festival, created in [[1863]] ({{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3), have been designated as cultural properties by the city of Kawachinagano. In [[1908]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 41), the shrine merged with the Spring Shrine (春日神社) from the Hino district, enshrining {{ill|Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|Futsunushi}}, and {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami}}. It also incorporated {{ill|Ōkuninushi}} from Yasaka Shrine. == Festivals == The shrine's parish covers the Takō and Hino districts of Kawachinagano. The annual festival takes place on October 10 and includes a children's lion dance, the Hino lion dance (a Kawachinagano City-designated intangible folk cultural property), and the ceremonial entry of {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} floats into the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Kawachinagano City-Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Main Hall**: Constructed in the early Edo period, designated on March 30, 1976. * **Wooden Statues**: Includes a goddess, Gozu Tennō, and a male deity. The goddess and Gozu Tennō statues date back to the Kamakura period, while the male deity statue is from the Muromachi period. Designated on March 27, 1970. * **Takō Shrine Festival Ema**: Created in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), designated on March 19, 1982. === Kawachinagano City-Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * **Hino Lion Dance**: A practice dating back to the Edo period, designated on March 29, 1984. == Access == ;Location * 291 Takō, Kawachinagano, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ;Transportation * The nearest station is {{ill|Kawachinagano Station|ja|河内長野駅}}, accessible via the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線}}. From the station, take the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス}} Takō Line and get off at the "Kamidakō" bus stop. The shrine is about a 3-minute walk south (approximately 230 meters). == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_02_kawatinagano_takou.html Takō Shrine - Osaka Shrine Association (大阪府神社庁)] {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kawachinagano]] [[Category:completion tier 3]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]] [[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:高向神社]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

The shrine is about a 3-minute walk south (approximately 230 meters). == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_02_kawatinagano_takou.html Takō Shrine - Osaka Shrine Association (大阪府神社庁)] {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kawachinagano]] [[Category:completion tier 3]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]] [[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:高向神社]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=高向神社 |image=[[File:Takou-jinja1.jpg|250px]]
社殿正面 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[河内長野市]][[高向]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 26 | 緯度秒 =8 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 32 | 経度秒 = 56.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Osaka |祭神=[[素盞嗚尊]]
[[蛭子神]]
[[天児屋根命]]
[[保食神]]
[[菊理媛神|白山姫命]]
[[Sugawara no Michizane]] |社格= |創建=不詳 |本殿=三間社[[切妻造]] |別名= 五社明神（旧称） |札所等= |例祭=10月10日に獅子舞、地車 |神事= }} '''Takō Shrine''' (高向神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. [[ja:高向神社_(河内長野市)]] == Deities Enshrined == The shrine originally enshrined {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子神}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食神}}, {{ill|Kukurihime|ja|菊理媛神}} (Shirayama-hime no Mikoto), and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}. Later, the shrine incorporated additional deities from nearby shrines, including {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami|ja|比咩大神}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大巳貴命}}. == History == The exact year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. The main hall was constructed in [[1608]] ({{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 13) and features a three-bay gabled roof style (三間社切妻造). The main hall and a votive ema depicting the Takō Shrine festival, created in [[1863]] ({{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3), have been designated as cultural properties by the city of Kawachinagano. In [[1908]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 41), the shrine merged with the Spring Shrine (春日神社) from the Hino district, enshrining {{ill|Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|Futsunushi}}, and {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami}}. It also incorporated {{ill|Ōkuninushi}} from Yasaka Shrine. == Festivals == The shrine's parish covers the Takō and Hino districts of Kawachinagano. The annual festival takes place on October 10 and includes a children's lion dance, the Hino lion dance (a Kawachinagano City-designated intangible folk cultural property), and the ceremonial entry of {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} floats into the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Kawachinagano City-Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Main Hall''': Constructed in the early Edo period, designated on March 30, 1976. * '''Wooden Statues''': Includes a goddess, Gozu Tennō, and a male deity. The goddess and Gozu Tennō statues date back to the Kamakura period, while the male deity statue is from the Muromachi period. Designated on March 27, 1970. * '''Takō Shrine Festival Ema''': Created in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), designated on March 19, 1982. === Kawachinagano City-Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Hino Lion Dance''': A practice dating back to the Edo period, designated on March 29, 1984. == Access == ;Location * 291 Takō, Kawachinagano, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ;Transportation * The nearest station is {{ill|Kawachinagano Station|ja|河内長野駅}}, accessible via the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線}}. From the station, take the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス}} Takō Line and get off at the "Kamidakō" bus stop. The shrine is about a 3-minute walk south (approximately 230 meters). == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_02_kawatinagano_takou.html Takō Shrine - Osaka Shrine Association (大阪府神社庁)] {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kawachinagano]] [[Category:completion tier 3]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]] [[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:高向神社]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]

{{神社 |名称=高向神社 |image=[[File:Takou-jinja1.jpg|250px]]
社殿正面 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[河内長野市]][[高向]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 26 | 緯度秒 =8 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 32 | 経度秒 = 56.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Osaka |祭神=[[素盞嗚尊]]
[[蛭子神]]
[[天児屋根命]]
[[保食神]]
[[菊理媛神|白山姫命]]
[[Sugawara no Michizane]] |社格= |創建=不詳 |本殿=三間社[[切妻造]] |別名= 五社明神（旧称） |札所等= |例祭=10月10日に獅子舞、地車 |神事= }} '''Takō Shrine''' (高向神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. [[ja:高向神社_(河内長野市)]] == Deities Enshrined == The shrine originally enshrined {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子神}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食神}}, {{ill|Kukurihime|ja|菊理媛神}} (Shirayama-hime no Mikoto), and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}. Later, the shrine incorporated additional deities from nearby shrines, including {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami|ja|比咩大神}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大巳貴命}}. == History == The exact year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. The main hall was constructed in [[1608]] ({{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 13) and features a three-bay gabled roof style (三間社切妻造). The main hall and a votive ema depicting the Takō Shrine festival, created in [[1863]] ({{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3), have been designated as cultural properties by the city of Kawachinagano. In [[1908]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 41), the shrine merged with the Spring Shrine (春日神社) from the Hino district, enshrining {{ill|Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|Futsunushi}}, and {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami}}. It also incorporated {{ill|Ōkuninushi}} from Yasaka Shrine. == Festivals == The shrine's parish covers the Takō and Hino districts of Kawachinagano. The annual festival takes place on October 10 and includes a children's lion dance, the Hino lion dance (a Kawachinagano City-designated intangible folk cultural property), and the ceremonial entry of {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} floats into the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Kawachinagano City-Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Main Hall''': Constructed in the early Edo period, designated on March 30, 1976. * '''Wooden Statues''': Includes a goddess, Gozu Tennō, and a male deity. The goddess and Gozu Tennō statues date back to the Kamakura period, while the male deity statue is from the Muromachi period. Designated on March 27, 1970. * '''Takō Shrine Festival Ema''': Created in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), designated on March 19, 1982. === Kawachinagano City-Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Hino Lion Dance''': A practice dating back to the Edo period, designated on March 29, 1984. == Access == ;Location * 291 Takō, Kawachinagano, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ;Transportation * The nearest station is {{ill|Kawachinagano Station|ja|河内長野駅}}, accessible via the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線}}. From the station, take the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス}} Takō Line and get off at the "Kamidakō" bus stop. The shrine is about a 3-minute walk south (approximately 230 meters). == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_02_kawatinagano_takou.html Takō Shrine - Osaka Shrine Association (大阪府神社庁)] {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kawachinagano]] [[Category:completion tier 3]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]] [[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:高向神社]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]]

The shrine is about a 3-minute walk south (approximately 230 meters). == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_02_kawatinagano_takou.html Takō Shrine - Osaka Shrine Association (大阪府神社庁)] {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kawachinagano]] [[Category:completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]] [[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:高向神社]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{神社 |名称=高向神社 |image=[[File:Takou-jinja1.jpg|250px]]
社殿正面 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[河内長野市]][[高向]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 26 | 緯度秒 =8 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 32 | 経度秒 = 56.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Osaka |祭神=[[素盞嗚尊]]
[[蛭子神]]
[[天児屋根命]]
[[保食神]]
[[菊理媛神|白山姫命]]
[[Sugawara no Michizane]] |社格= |創建=不詳 |本殿=三間社[[切妻造]] |別名= 五社明神（旧称） |札所等= |例祭=10月10日に獅子舞、地車 |神事= }} '''Takō Shrine''' (高向神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. [[ja:高向神社_(河内長野市)]] == Deities Enshrined == The shrine originally enshrined {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊}}, {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子神}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食神}}, {{ill|Kukurihime|ja|菊理媛神}} (Shirayama-hime no Mikoto), and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}. Later, the shrine incorporated additional deities from nearby shrines, including {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命}}, {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami|ja|比咩大神}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大巳貴命}}. == History == The exact year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. The main hall was constructed in [[1608]] ({{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 13) and features a three-bay gabled roof style (三間社切妻造). The main hall and a votive ema depicting the Takō Shrine festival, created in [[1863]] ({{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3), have been designated as cultural properties by the city of Kawachinagano. In [[1908]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 41), the shrine merged with the Spring Shrine (春日神社) from the Hino district, enshrining {{ill|Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|Futsunushi}}, and {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami}}. It also incorporated {{ill|Ōkuninushi}} from Yasaka Shrine. == Festivals == The shrine's parish covers the Takō and Hino districts of Kawachinagano. The annual festival takes place on October 10 and includes a children's lion dance, the Hino lion dance (a Kawachinagano City-designated intangible folk cultural property), and the ceremonial entry of {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車}} floats into the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Kawachinagano City-Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Main Hall''': Constructed in the early Edo period, designated on March 30, 1976. * '''Wooden Statues''': Includes a goddess, Gozu Tennō, and a male deity. The goddess and Gozu Tennō statues date back to the Kamakura period, while the male deity statue is from the Muromachi period. Designated on March 27, 1970. * '''Takō Shrine Festival Ema''': Created in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), designated on March 19, 1982. === Kawachinagano City-Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Hino Lion Dance''': A practice dating back to the Edo period, designated on March 29, 1984. == Access == ;Location * 291 Takō, Kawachinagano, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ;Transportation * The nearest station is {{ill|Kawachinagano Station|ja|河内長野駅}}, accessible via the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線}}. From the station, take the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス}} Takō Line and get off at the "Kamidakō" bus stop. The shrine is about a 3-minute walk south (approximately 230 meters). == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_02_kawatinagano_takou.html Takō Shrine - Osaka Shrine Association (大阪府神社庁)] {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kawachinagano]] [[Category:completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]] [[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:高向神社]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

Designated on March 27, 1970. * '''Takō Shrine Festival Ema''': Created in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久}} 3 (1863), designated on March 19, 1982. === Kawachinagano City-Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Hino Lion Dance''': A practice dating back to the Edo period, designated on March 29, 1984. == Access == ;Location * 291 Takō, Kawachinagano, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ;Transportation * The nearest station is {{ill|Kawachinagano Station|ja|河内長野駅}}, accessible via the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線}}. {{神社 |名称=高向神社 |image=[[File:Takou-jinja1.jpg|250px]]
社殿正面 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[河内長野市]][[高向]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 26 | 緯度秒 =8 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 32 | 経度秒 = 56.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Osaka |祭神=[[素盞嗚尊]]
[[蛭子神]]
[[天児屋根命]]
[[保食神]]
[[菊理媛神|白山姫命]]
[[Sugawara no Michizane]] |社格= |創建=不詳 |本殿=三間社[[切妻造]] |別名= 五社明神（旧称） |札所等= |例祭=10月10日に獅子舞、地車 |神事= }} '''Takō Shrine''' (高向神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}. == Deities Enshrined == The shrine originally enshrined {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kukurihime|ja|菊理媛神|12=draft}} (Shirayama-hime no Mikoto), and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}}. Later, the shrine incorporated additional deities from nearby shrines, including {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami|ja|比咩大神|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大巳貴命|12=draft}}. == History == The exact year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. The main hall was constructed in [[1608]] ({{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|12=draft}} 13) and features a three-bay gabled roof style (三間社切妻造). The main hall and a votive ema depicting the Takō Shrine festival, created in [[1863]] ({{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久|12=draft}} 3), have been designated as cultural properties by the city of Kawachinagano. In [[1908]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 41), the shrine merged with the Spring Shrine (春日神社) from the Hino district, enshrining {{ill|Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|Futsunushi}}, and {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami|12=draft}}. It also incorporated {{ill|Ōkuninushi}} from Yasaka Shrine. == Festivals == The shrine's parish covers the Takō and Hino districts of Kawachinagano. The annual festival takes place on October 10 and includes a children's lion dance, the Hino lion dance (a Kawachinagano City-designated intangible folk cultural property), and the ceremonial entry of {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車|12=draft}} floats into the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Kawachinagano City-Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Main Hall''': Constructed in the early Edo period, designated on March 30, 1976. * '''Wooden Statues''': Includes a goddess, Gozu Tennō, and a male deity. The goddess and Gozu Tennō statues date back to the Kamakura period, while the male deity statue is from the Muromachi period. Designated on March 27, 1970. * '''Takō Shrine Festival Ema''': Created in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久|12=draft}} 3 (1863), designated on March 19, 1982. === Kawachinagano City-Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Hino Lion Dance''': A practice dating back to the Edo period, designated on March 29, 1984. == Access == ;Location * 291 Takō, Kawachinagano, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ;Transportation * The nearest station is {{ill|Kawachinagano Station|ja|河内長野駅|12=draft}}, accessible via the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線|12=draft}}. From the station, take the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス|12=draft}} Takō Line and get off at the "Kamidakō" bus stop. The shrine is about a 3-minute walk south (approximately 230 meters). == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_02_kawatinagano_takou.html Takō Shrine - Osaka Shrine Association (大阪府神社庁)] {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kawachinagano]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:高向神社_(河内長野市)]] [[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]] [[ja:高向神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)}}

The main hall was constructed in [[1608]] ({{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|12=draft}} 13) and features a three-bay gabled roof style (三間社切妻造). The main hall and a votive ema depicting the Takō Shrine festival, created in [[1863]] ({{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久|12=draft}} 3), have been designated as cultural properties by the city of Kawachinagano. In [[1908]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 41), the shrine merged with the Spring Shrine (春日神社) from the Hino district, enshrining {{ill|Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|Futsunushi}}, and {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami|12=draft}}. It also incorporated {{ill|Ōkuninushi}} from Yasaka Shrine. == Festivals == The shrine's parish covers the Takō and Hino districts of Kawachinagano. The annual festival takes place on October 10 and includes a children's lion dance, the Hino lion dance (a Kawachinagano City-designated intangible folk cultural property), and the ceremonial entry of {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車|12=draft}} floats into the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Kawachinagano City-Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Main Hall''': Constructed in the early Edo period, designated on March 30, 1976. * '''Wooden Statues''': Includes a goddess, Gozu Tennō, and a male deity. The goddess and Gozu Tennō statues date back to the Kamakura period, while the male deity statue is from the Muromachi period. Designated on March 27, 1970. * '''Takō Shrine Festival Ema''': Created in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久|12=draft}} 3 (1863), designated on March 19, 1982. === Kawachinagano City-Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Hino Lion Dance''': A practice dating back to the Edo period, designated on March 29, 1984. == Access == ;Location * 291 Takō, Kawachinagano, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ;Transportation * The nearest station is {{ill|Kawachinagano Station|ja|河内長野駅|12=draft}}, accessible via the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線|12=draft}}. From the station, take the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス|12=draft}} Takō Line and get off at the "Kamidakō" bus stop. The shrine is about a 3-minute walk south (approximately 230 meters). == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_02_kawatinagano_takou.html Takō Shrine - Osaka Shrine Association (大阪府神社庁)] {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:高向神社_(河内長野市)]] [[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]] [[ja:高向神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)}}

Designated on March 27, 1970. * '''Takō Shrine Festival Ema''': Created in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (1863), designated on March 19, 1982. === Kawachinagano City-Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Hino Lion Dance''': A practice dating back to the Edo period, designated on March 29, 1984. == Access == ;Location * 291 Takō, Kawachinagano, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ;Transportation * The nearest station is {{ill|Kawachinagano Station|ja|河内長野駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, accessible via the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. From the station, take the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Takō Line and get off at the "Kamidakō" bus stop. The shrine is about a 3-minute walk south (approximately 230 meters). == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_02_kawatinagano_takou.html Takō Shrine - Osaka Shrine Association (大阪府神社庁)] {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:高向神社_(河内長野市)]] [[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]] [[ja:高向神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)}}

revision.comment: 12 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:高向神社 (河内長野市) up to rev 82550896
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=高向神社 |画像=[[File:Takou-jinja1.jpg|250px]]
社殿正面 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[河内長野市]][[高向]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 26 | 緯度秒 =8 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 32 | 経度秒 = 56.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Osaka |祭神=[[素盞嗚尊]]
[[蛭子神]]
[[天児屋根命]]
[[保食神]]
[[菊理媛神|白山姫命]]
[[菅原道真]] |社格= |創建=不詳 |本殿=三間社[[切妻造]] |別名= 五社明神（旧称） |札所等= |例祭=10月10日に獅子舞、地車 |神事= }} '''高向神社'''（たこうじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[河内長野市]]にある神社。 ==祭神== 祭神は、当初、[[素盞嗚尊]]、[[蛭子神]]、[[天児屋根命]]、[[保食神]]、[[菊理媛神|白山姫命]]、[[菅原道真]]。
現在は近隣の神社から[[武甕槌命]]、[[経津主命]]、[[比咩大神]]、[[大巳貴命]]が合祀されている。 ==歴史== 創建年は不詳。本殿は[[1608年]]（[[慶長]]13年）の建立で三間社切妻造。本殿と[[1863年]]（[[文久]]3年）に作られた高向神社祭礼図絵馬は河内長野市の市指定文化財に指定されている。[[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）に日野地区にあった春日神社の[[武甕槌命]]、[[経津主命]]、[[比咩大神]]と八坂神社の[[大巳貴命]]が合祀された。 == 祭事 == 氏地は河内長野市[[高向|高向、日野]]地区である。例大祭は10月10日で、こども獅子舞、日野獅子舞（河内長野市無形民俗文化財）、[[地車]]の宮入が行われる。 == 文化財 == ;河内長野市指定有形民俗文化財 *高向神社本殿（江戸時代初期の建立） - 昭和51年3月30日指定 *女神、牛頭天王、男神の各坐像（木造・女神と牛頭天王は鎌倉期の作、男神は室町期の作） - 昭和45年3月27日指定 *高向神社祭礼図絵馬（文久3年の作） - 昭和57年3月19日指定 ;河内長野市指定無形民俗文化財 *日野地区獅子舞（江戸時代から） - 昭和59年3月29日指定 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[河内長野市]][[高向]]291番地 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[南海高野線]]及び[[近鉄長野線]][[河内長野駅]]下車後[[南海バス]]高向線に乗車し、バス停「上高向」下車から徒歩約3分（南へ約230m） == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_02_kawatinagano_takou.html 高向神社 - 大阪府神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たこうしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内長野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内長野市の建築物]]

[[蛭子神]]
[[天児屋根命]]
[[保食神]]
[[菊理媛神|白山姫命]]
[[菅原道真]] |社格= |創建=不詳 |本殿=三間社[[切妻造]] |別名= 五社明神（旧称） |札所等= |例祭=10月10日に獅子舞、地車 |神事= }} '''高向神社'''（たこうじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[河内長野市]]にある神社。 ==祭神== 祭神は、当初、[[素盞嗚尊]]、[[蛭子神]]、[[天児屋根命]]、[[保食神]]、[[菊理媛神|白山姫命]]、[[菅原道真]]。
現在は近隣の神社から[[武甕槌命]]、[[経津主命]]、[[比咩大神]]、[[大巳貴命]]が合祀されている。 ==歴史== 創建年は不詳。本殿は[[1608年]]（[[慶長]]13年）の建立で三間社切妻造。本殿と[[1863年]]（[[文久]]3年）に作られた高向神社祭礼図絵馬は河内長野市の市指定文化財に指定されている。[[1908年]]（[[明治]]41年）に日野地区にあった春日神社の[[武甕槌命]]、[[経津主命]]、[[比咩大神]]と八坂神社の[[大巳貴命]]が合祀された。 == 祭事 == 氏地は河内長野市[[高向|高向、日野]]地区である。例大祭は10月10日で、こども獅子舞、日野獅子舞（河内長野市無形民俗文化財）、[[地車]]の宮入が行われる。 == 文化財 == ;河内長野市指定有形民俗文化財 *高向神社本殿（江戸時代初期の建立） - 昭和51年3月30日指定 *女神、牛頭天王、男神の各坐像（木造・女神と牛頭天王は鎌倉期の作、男神は室町期の作） - 昭和45年3月27日指定 *高向神社祭礼図絵馬（文久3年の作） - 昭和57年3月19日指定 ;河内長野市指定無形民俗文化財 *日野地区獅子舞（江戸時代から） - 昭和59年3月29日指定 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[河内長野市]][[高向]]291番地 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[南海高野線]]及び[[近鉄長野線]][[河内長野駅]]下車後[[南海バス]]高向線に乗車し、バス停「上高向」下車から徒歩約3分（南へ約230m） == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_02_kawatinagano_takou.html 高向神社 - 大阪府神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たこうしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内長野市の歴史]] [[Category:河内長野市の建築物]]

{{神社 |名称=高向神社 |image=[[File:Takou-jinja1.jpg|250px]]
社殿正面 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[河内長野市]][[高向]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 26 | 緯度秒 =8 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 32 | 経度秒 = 56.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Osaka |祭神=[[素盞嗚尊]]
[[蛭子神]]
[[天児屋根命]]
[[保食神]]
[[菊理媛神|白山姫命]]
[[Sugawara no Michizane]] |社格= |創建=不詳 |本殿=三間社[[切妻造]] |別名= 五社明神（旧称） |札所等= |例祭=10月10日に獅子舞、地車 |神事= }} '''Takō Shrine''' (高向神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities Enshrined == The shrine originally enshrined {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ukemochi|ja|保食神|1=Ukemochi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kukurihime|ja|菊理媛神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shirayama-hime no Mikoto), and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Later, the shrine incorporated additional deities from nearby shrines, including {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami|ja|比咩大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大巳貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == The exact year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. The main hall was constructed in [[1608]] ({{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 13) and features a three-bay gabled roof style (三間社切妻造). The main hall and a votive ema depicting the Takō Shrine festival, created in [[1863]] ({{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3), have been designated as cultural properties by the city of Kawachinagano. In [[1908]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 41), the shrine merged with the Spring Shrine (春日神社) from the Hino district, enshrining {{ill|Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|Futsunushi}}, and {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami|qq=draft}}. It also incorporated {{ill|Ōkuninushi}} from Yasaka Shrine. == Festivals == The shrine's parish covers the Takō and Hino districts of Kawachinagano. The annual festival takes place on October 10 and includes a children's lion dance, the Hino lion dance (a Kawachinagano City-designated intangible folk cultural property), and the ceremonial entry of {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車|qq=draft}} floats into the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Kawachinagano City-Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Main Hall''': Constructed in the early Edo period, designated on March 30, 1976. * '''Wooden Statues''': Includes a goddess, Gozu Tennō, and a male deity. The goddess and Gozu Tennō statues date back to the Kamakura period, while the male deity statue is from the Muromachi period. Designated on March 27, 1970. * '''Takō Shrine Festival Ema''': Created in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (1863), designated on March 19, 1982. === Kawachinagano City-Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Hino Lion Dance''': A practice dating back to the Edo period, designated on March 29, 1984. == Access == ;Location * 291 Takō, Kawachinagano, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ;Transportation * The nearest station is {{ill|Kawachinagano Station|ja|河内長野駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, accessible via the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. From the station, take the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Takō Line and get off at the "Kamidakō" bus stop. The shrine is about a 3-minute walk south (approximately 230 meters). == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_02_kawatinagano_takou.html Takō Shrine - Osaka Shrine Association (大阪府神社庁)] {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:高向神社_(河内長野市)]] [[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]] [[ja:高向神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)}} {{translated page|ja|高向神社 (河内長野市)|version=82550896|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It also incorporated {{ill|Ōkuninushi}} from Yasaka Shrine. == Festivals == The shrine's parish covers the Takō and Hino districts of Kawachinagano. The annual festival takes place on October 10 and includes a children's lion dance, the Hino lion dance (a Kawachinagano City-designated intangible folk cultural property), and the ceremonial entry of {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車|qq=draft}} floats into the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Kawachinagano City-Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Main Hall''': Constructed in the early Edo period, designated on March 30, 1976. * '''Wooden Statues''': Includes a goddess, Gozu Tennō, and a male deity. The goddess and Gozu Tennō statues date back to the Kamakura period, while the male deity statue is from the Muromachi period. Designated on March 27, 1970. * '''Takō Shrine Festival Ema''': Created in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (1863), designated on March 19, 1982. === Kawachinagano City-Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Hino Lion Dance''': A practice dating back to the Edo period, designated on March 29, 1984. == Access == ;Location * 291 Takō, Kawachinagano, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ;Transportation * The nearest station is {{ill|Kawachinagano Station|ja|河内長野駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, accessible via the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. From the station, take the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Takō Line and get off at the "Kamidakō" bus stop. The shrine is about a 3-minute walk south (approximately 230 meters). == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_02_kawatinagano_takou.html Takō Shrine - Osaka Shrine Association (大阪府神社庁)] {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{translated page|ja|高向神社 (河内長野市)|version=82550896|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:高向神社_(河内長野市)]] [[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]] [[ja:高向神社]]

{{神社 |名称=高向神社 |image=[[File:Takou-jinja1.jpg|250px]]
社殿正面 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[河内長野市]][[高向]] | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 26 | 緯度秒 =8 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 32 | 経度秒 = 56.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Osaka |祭神=[[素盞嗚尊]]

From the station, take the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Takō Line and get off at the "Kamidakō" bus stop. The shrine is about a 3-minute walk south (approximately 230 meters). == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_02_kawatinagano_takou.html Takō Shrine - Osaka Shrine Association (大阪府神社庁)] {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{translated page|ja|高向神社 (河内長野市)|version=82550896|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:高向神社_(河内長野市)]] [[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]] [[ja:高向神社]]

The annual festival takes place on October 10 and includes a children's lion dance, the Hino lion dance (a Kawachinagano City-designated intangible folk cultural property), and the ceremonial entry of {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjiri|qq=}} floats into the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Kawachinagano City-Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Main Hall''': Constructed in the early Edo period, designated on March 30, 1976. * '''Wooden Statues''': Includes a goddess, Gozu Tennō, and a male deity. The goddess and Gozu Tennō statues date back to the Kamakura period, while the male deity statue is from the Muromachi period. Designated on March 27, 1970. * '''Takō Shrine Festival Ema''': Created in {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū|qq=}} 3 (1863), designated on March 19, 1982. === Kawachinagano City-Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Hino Lion Dance''': A practice dating back to the Edo period, designated on March 29, 1984. == Access == ;Location * 291 Takō, Kawachinagano, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ;Transportation * The nearest station is {{ill|Kawachinagano Station|ja|河内長野駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachinagano Station|qq=}}, accessible via the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nankai Kōya Line|qq=}} and {{ill|Kintetsu Nagano Line|ja|近鉄長野線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Nagano Line|qq=}}. From the station, take the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nankai Bus|qq=}} Takō Line and get off at the "Kamidakō" bus stop. The shrine is about a 3-minute walk south (approximately 230 meters). == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_02_kawatinagano_takou.html Takō Shrine - Osaka Shrine Association (大阪府神社庁)] {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{translated page|ja|高向神社 (河内長野市)|version=82550896|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]] }} [[ja:高向神社_(河内長野市)]] [[sn:Takamuku Shrine (Kawachinagano)]] [[ja:高向神社]]
