{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |社格= |創建=不詳 |本殿=三間社[[切妻造]] |別名= 五社明神（旧称） |札所等= |例祭=10月10日に獅子舞、地車 |神事= |prefecture={{ill|Kawachinagano|WD=Q819572|1=Kawachinagano}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.43556|N|135.549056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Takō Shrine''' (高向神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|en|Kawachinagano|WD=Q819572|lt=Kawachinagano|1=Kawachinagano}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. == Deities Enshrined == The shrine originally enshrined {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒルコ|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi|1=Ukemochi}}, {{ill|Kukurihime|en|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime|1=Kukurihime}} (Shirayama-hime no Mikoto), and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. Later, the shrine incorporated additional deities from nearby shrines, including {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}}, {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami|ja|比咩大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大巳貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == History == The exact year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. The main hall was constructed in {{ill|1608|en|1608|WD=Q6758|lt=1608|1=1608}} ({{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 13) and features a three-bay gabled roof style (三間社切妻造). The main hall and a votive ema depicting the Takō Shrine festival, created in {{ill|1863|en|1863|WD=Q7701|lt=1863|1=1863}} ({{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū|1=Bunkyū}} 3), have been designated as cultural properties by the city of Kawachinagano. In {{ill|1908|en|1908|WD=Q2056|lt=1908|1=1908}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 41), the shrine merged with the Spring Shrine (春日神社) from the Hino district, enshrining {{ill|Takemikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=}}. It also incorporated {{ill|Ōkuninushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} from Yasaka Shrine. == Festivals == The shrine's parish covers the Takō and Hino districts of Kawachinagano. The annual festival takes place on October 10 and includes a children's lion dance, the Hino lion dance (a Kawachinagano City-designated intangible folk cultural property), and the ceremonial entry of {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjiri|1=danjiri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424706}} floats into the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Kawachinagano City-Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Main Hall''': Constructed in the early Edo period, designated on March 30, 1976. * '''Wooden Statues''': Includes a goddess, Gozu Tennō, and a male deity. The goddess and Gozu Tennō statues date back to the Kamakura period, while the male deity statue is from the Muromachi period. Designated on March 27, 1970. * '''Takō Shrine Festival Ema''': Created in {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū|1=Bunkyū}} 3 (1863), designated on March 19, 1982. === Kawachinagano City-Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Hino Lion Dance''': A practice dating back to the Edo period, designated on March 29, 1984. == Access == ;Location * 291 Takō, Kawachinagano, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ;Transportation * The nearest station is {{ill|Kawachinagano Station|en|Kawachinagano Station|WD=Q6379476|lt=Kawachinagano Station|1=Kawachinagano Station}}, accessible via the {{ill|Koya Line|en|Koya Line|WD=Q71986|lt=Nankai Kōya Line|1=Koya Line}} and {{ill|Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|en|Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q901413|lt=Kintetsu Nagano Line|1=Nagano Line (Kintetsu)}}. From the station, take the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nankai Bus|1=Nankai Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408154}} Takō Line and get off at the "Kamidakō" bus stop. The shrine is about a 3-minute walk south (approximately 230 meters). == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_02_kawatinagano_takou.html Takō Shrine - Osaka Shrine Association (大阪府神社庁)] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|高向神社 (河内長野市)|version=82550896|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:高向神社_(河内長野市)]] [[ja:高向神社]] [[ja:高向神社 (河内長野市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11668969}} [[Category:Buildings in Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} |社格= |創建=不詳 |本殿=三間社[[切妻造]] |別名= 五社明神（旧称） |札所等= |例祭=10月10日に獅子舞、地車 |神事= |prefecture={{ill|Kawachinagano|WD=Q819572|1=Kawachinagano}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.43556|N|135.549056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Takō Shrine''' (高向神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|en|Kawachinagano|WD=Q819572|lt=Kawachinagano|1=Kawachinagano}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. == Deities Enshrined == The shrine originally enshrined {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒルコ|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}, {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi|1=Ukemochi}}, {{ill|Kikurihime|en|Kikurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kikurihime|1=Kikurihime}} (Shirayama-hime no Mikoto), and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. Later, the shrine incorporated additional deities from nearby shrines, including {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経津主神}}, {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami|ja|比咩大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大巳貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. == History == The exact year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. The main hall was constructed in {{ill|1608|en|1608|WD=Q6758|lt=1608|1=1608}} ({{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 13) and features a three-bay gabled roof style (三間社切妻造). The main hall and a votive ema depicting the Takō Shrine festival, created in {{ill|1863|en|1863|WD=Q7701|lt=1863|1=1863}} ({{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū|1=Bunkyū}} 3), have been designated as cultural properties by the city of Kawachinagano. In {{ill|1908|en|1908|WD=Q2056|lt=1908|1=1908}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 41), the shrine merged with the Spring Shrine (春日神社) from the Hino district, enshrining {{ill|Takemikazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Futsunushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Himegami|lt=Hime-no-Okami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=}}. It also incorporated {{ill|Ōkuninushi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} from Yasaka Shrine. == Festivals == The shrine's parish covers the Takō and Hino districts of Kawachinagano. The annual festival takes place on October 10 and includes a children's lion dance, the Hino lion dance (a Kawachinagano City-designated intangible folk cultural property), and the ceremonial entry of {{ill|Danjiri|ja|地車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danjiri|1=danjiri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424706}} floats into the shrine. == Cultural Properties == === Kawachinagano City-Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * '''Main Hall''': Constructed in the early Edo period, designated on March 30, 1976. * '''Wooden Statues''': Includes a goddess, Gozu Tennō, and a male deity. The goddess and Gozu Tennō statues date back to the Kamakura period, while the male deity statue is from the Muromachi period. Designated on March 27, 1970. * '''Takō Shrine Festival Ema''': Created in {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū|1=Bunkyū}} 3 (1863), designated on March 19, 1982. === Kawachinagano City-Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Hino Lion Dance''': A practice dating back to the Edo period, designated on March 29, 1984. == Access == ;Location * 291 Takō, Kawachinagano, Osaka Prefecture, Japan ;Transportation * The nearest station is {{ill|Kawachinagano Station|en|Kawachinagano Station|WD=Q6379476|lt=Kawachinagano Station|1=Kawachinagano Station}}, accessible via the {{ill|Koya Line|en|Koya Line|WD=Q71986|lt=Nankai Kōya Line|1=Koya Line}} and {{ill|Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|en|Nagano Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q901413|lt=Kintetsu Nagano Line|1=Nagano Line (Kintetsu)}}. From the station, take the {{ill|Nankai Bus|ja|南海バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nankai Bus|1=Nankai Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408154}} Takō Line and get off at the "Kamidakō" bus stop. The shrine is about a 3-minute walk south (approximately 230 meters). == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/kawatinagano-city/m17_02_kawatinagano_takou.html Takō Shrine - Osaka Shrine Association (大阪府神社庁)] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|高向神社 (河内長野市)|version=82550896|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:高向神社_(河内長野市)]] [[ja:高向神社]] [[ja:高向神社 (河内長野市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11668969}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kawachinagano]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
